sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The image still shows the same layout - purple on top, white on bottom. The DOWN action didn't seem to move the purple block visibly (or the image is cached). Let me check the actual frame data to see what changed.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after DOWN
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('p', 'W') and n['pixels'] > 20:
        print(f"Node {n['id']}: color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Compare with before
if last_transition and last_transition.before_frame:
    before_seg = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation
    for n in before_seg['nodes']:
        if n['color'] in ('p', 'W') and n['pixels'] > 20:
            print(f"BEFORE - Node {n['id']}: color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The purple block didn't move at all after DOWN! The boundaries are identical before and after. But the board_changed flag was true. Let me check what actually changed - probably just the timer/progress bar at the bottom.
So DOWN didn't move the purple block. But RIGHT did (it moved it diagonally and changed its shape). Let me think about this...
Maybe the purple block can only move in certain directions, or maybe there's a constraint. When I pressed RIGHT, it moved diagonally down-right. Maybe the block moves in the direction that's "open" or towards empty space.
Let me try UP to see if that moves it.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# DOWN didn't move the purple block - boundaries are identical
# Only RIGHT moved it (diagonally). Let me check what changed
if last_transition and last_transition.before_frame:
    before_seg = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation
    after_seg = current_frame.segmentation
    
    before_nodes = {n['hash']: n for n in before_seg['nodes']}
    after_nodes = {n['hash']: n for n in after_seg['nodes']}
    
    # Check all nodes for changes
    for n in after_seg['nodes']:
        if n['hash'] in before_nodes:
            bn = before_nodes[n['hash']]
            if bn['boundary'] != n['boundary'] or bn['pixels'] != n['pixels']:
                print(f"Changed: color={n['color']}, pixels {bn['pixels']}->{n['pixels']}")
        else:
            print(f"New node: color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Try UP
action(['UP'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
